docs.title: mininpu-compiler
docs.text: mininpu-compiler is a minimal, extensible compiler framework that lowers Triton kernels to a custom NPU ISA. It pairs a small, deliberately compact instruction set with a functional simulator (ISS), and targets arbitrary-size tiled GEMM, FlashAttention forward, and the building blocks of GPT-2 / LLaMA-style transformer blocks.

docs.text:

  • Compiler Architecture — end-to-end walkthrough of how a Triton kernel becomes NPU assembly, and what bounds lowerability.
  • Supported Triton — the authoritative record of what the compiler can and cannot lower today (compile-surface SSOT).

docs.title: Compiler Architecture
docs.text: End-to-end walkthrough of the npu-compiler backend: how a Triton kernel becomes NPU assembly, and the structural constraints that bound what is lowerable. This is the architecture SSOT; it cross-links (does not duplicate) the surface contract in SUPPORTED_TRITON.md, the milestone ladder in charter.md, and the ISA narrative in ../isa/npu-isa.md (opcode/encoding SSOT: sibling mininpu/isa/opcodes.py).

docs.title: 1. Pipeline
docs.text:
Triton (.py)
  -> triton-opt + triton-shared-opt          (triton-shared MLIR frontend)
2_linalg.mlir
  -> npu-opt --linalg-to-npu                 (LinalgToNPU.cpp)   [only conversion pass]
  -> npu-opt  PhysAllocPass                   (physical bank / pool assignment)
3_npu.mlir
  -> npu-translate --to-asm                   (NPUToAsm.cpp)      [serialization]
4_npu.s
  -> mininpu.asm parse_asm() -> mininpu.iss.simulator Simulator

mininpu_compiler/compile.py :: compile_kernel(fn, tiles) drives all stages. Per-stage snapshots are checked in under test/e2e/pipeline/{matmul,flash_attn}/. These .s snapshots are geometry-dependent and compiled-in (see §5): regenerating them requires a C++ rebuild, not just re-running the Python driver.

docs.title: 2. Two-level tiling model
docs.text:
Tiling is expressed at two levels and they must agree:

  • Program tiling - the Triton grid / tiles argument chooses the per-program tile shape and the number of program IDs (pid). Loop trip counts derived from problem size are literal in the emitted .s and are patched host-side per-pid (see §6).
  • Hardware tiling - each program's tile is further decomposed to the DIM x DIM systolic array and the 32 KiB unified pool. This decomposition is fixed by the compiled-in geometry (§5), not by the kernel.

Arbitrary problem sizes are handled by looping the fixed hardware tile, not by re-parameterizing the array.

docs.title: 3. Conversion: LinalgToNPU (the only conversion pass)
docs.text:
LinalgToNPU.cpp is the sole linalg->npu conversion pass. Its patterns are deliberately narrow:

  • Almost every pattern calls getSingleBodyOp() and matches exactly one arith/math op in the region body. Single-op elementwise/reduction bodies lower cleanly through the pattern set.
  • Multi-op bodies bypass the clean patterns. Fused activations whose body is several ops (GELU, LayerNorm, SiLU/SwiGLU, RMSNorm) do not match a single-op pattern and are instead reconstructed later in the serializer's fold matcher (§5). This is the root of the extensibility debt tracked in issues #10-#12: GELU is the same class as SiLU; LayerNorm is RMSNorm's fold extended with a mean-subtraction term.

docs.title: 4. Address calculation: LinearAddrAnalysis
docs.text:
Address generation lives in LinearAddrAnalysis.cpp (renamed from the older LinearAddr analysis). It computes linearized DMA/pool addresses from loop induction variables.

Hard constraint: induction-variable nesting is capped at iv <= 1. Address generation supports at most one nested induction variable, which bounds the loop-nest depth an address expression can span. New ops or tilings that would require deeper affine address nesting are not lowerable without extending this analysis. Treat iv <= 1 as a first-class limit when scoping new kernels.

docs.title: 5. Physical allocation + serialization
docs.text:
PhysAllocPass assigns physical banks / pool offsets. Geometry is compiled into the binary: PhysAllocPass::getPhysProfile() hardcodes named profiles (historically tutorial dim4 / bench_n8 / prod dim16). Changing geometry therefore requires editing C++ and make build, and then regenerating the pipeline .s snapshots. The single fixed shipped geometry (DIM=8, one 32 KiB pool) is described in the root CLAUDE.md hardware-geometry table; mininpu/hwparams.py in the sibling package is the numeric SSOT.

NPUToAsm.cpp serialization is a plain in-order IR-walk - there is NO instruction scheduler. Instructions are emitted in program order; there is no reordering, no software pipelining, and no double-buffering. The flags[5]=async DMA bit is not exploited (it is a functional no-op in the current sim). This is the central efficiency limitation; the design rationale and the planned scheduling + async-DMA pass are in charter.md § Efficiency (design notes).

Two concrete debts inside NPUToAsm.cpp (context for issues #10-#12):

  • Stateful fold matcher (~360 lines, approx L709-1073). Multi-op activation bodies (§3) are reconstructed here via five order-dependent state maps: fillConsts, siluSrcOf, scaleAddOf, sqrtMarked, broadcastSrc. This rebuilds SiLU/RMSNorm from the serialized stream. Adding GELU/LayerNorm means extending these state maps, not adding a clean pattern.
  • Mirrored dyn_cast chains. translateOp and walkOp each carry ~23 hand-mirrored dyn_cast branches that must be kept in sync manually (the file header admits this). #12 proposes generating them from NPU.td.

docs.title: 6. Host-patch seam (loop bounds)
docs.text: Loop trip counts are literal-only in the emitted assembly. Problem-size- and pid-dependent bounds are resolved by the host at launch via an ABI patch table: the host writes per-pid loop bounds / kernel-arg CSRs before dispatch (e.g. loop.begin.csr reads kernel_args[csr_idx]). The compiler does not emit dynamic bound arithmetic; it emits placeholders + a patch descriptor. This is why a wrong or omitted compile-time scalar (e.g. the VALID_CTX / N_CTX_TILES class of bug, issue #6) silently lowers to an unset runtime arg rather than a compile error.

docs.title: 7. Structural limits (what bounds new work)
docs.text:
• Single-op body matching in LinalgToNPU (§3) - multi-op ops fall to the fold matcher.
  • iv <= 1 address-nesting cap in LinearAddrAnalysis (§4).
  • Geometry compiled-in via PhysAllocPass (§5) - geometry changes need a rebuild + snapshot regen.
  • No scheduler / no double-buffering / async-DMA unused (§5) - correctness-first, efficiency deferred to v2.

docs.title: 8. Packaging & external consumption
docs.text:
Outside this repo the compiler is consumed, never built from source: consumers install a self-contained wheel (scripts/build-release.sh), which decouples them from the heavy MLIR build and the Triton pin. Release facts worth preserving:

  • Default deliverable is a linux_x86_64 wheel (~40 MB stripped, fits PyPI's 100 MB limit): npu-opt/npu-translate statically link MLIR/LLVM/Triton, bundle libstdc++/libgcc_s, and set RPATH=$ORIGIN/../lib (zero LD_LIBRARY_PATH).
  • No manylinux tag is emittable. An earlier setup.py hand-asserted manylinux_2_28; that was FALSE - auditwheel proves the binaries require GLIBC_2.34 and are x86_64_v2, so a 2_28-tagged wheel installs on RHEL8 and then crashes. auditwheel is the truth-teller, not a gate. A manylinux build is only reachable via local docker (scripts/build-release-docker.sh) + auditwheel repair. The [frontend] extra pins triton==3.4.0.
  • Ecosystem (github.com/sunwookim028), for cross-repo context only: mininpu = RTL/verification/FPGA and the Python package that is the ISA SSOT (file ISA issues there; Discussions are OFF, use Issues); mininpu-sdk/mininpu-runtime = host driver (NpuDriver); mininpu-compiler = this repo; mininpu-inference (ex mininpu-software) = Triton kernel suite + LLM integration + tutorial arc; mininpu-labs is RETIRING (absorbed into mininpu). Triton is pinned to 3.4.0 ecosystem-wide (triton-shared built against triton e44bd1c8).

docs.title: 9. Design positioning (production analogs)
docs.text:
The backend's structural choices are the production-default for accelerator compilers, not minimal shortcuts. The named analogs are the rationale for each shape:

  • CISC matmul.tile macro over RISC-shaped vector ops mirrors the TPU (one MXU matmul opcode wraps the inner DIM-loop; surrounding ops are RISC) and Triton's tl.dot. The tile is the hardware loop unit, so the inner array loop is never exposed to software. flash_attn.tile-style CISC macros are a logical extension of the same design.
  • Three on-chip spaces (SPM bf16 / ACC fp32 / VREG) match the TPU's VMEM/SMEM/CMEM split. Three is the production minimum: fewer would force precision conversions, more would need extra DMA. fp32 accumulation is required for numerical stability across long K. (These are dtype views of one unified byte-pool, per §5.)
  • Out-of-tree MLIR npu dialect (§3, §5) is the production-default for new accelerator compilers - IREE, Triton, Mojo, Cerebras CSL, and Tenstorrent TT-Forge are all out-of-tree MLIR - reusing the upstream pattern rewriter, type system, and linalg/scf/memref dialects.
  • Online softmax decomposed at the linalg level (explicit rowmax / sub / exp2 / scale / fma generics) matches how Triton, JAX Pallas, and Mojo express attention; the decomposed form is the right granularity for a vector ISA. The absence of RISC-level fusion across those vector ops is the efficiency gap in charter.md § Efficiency (issue #25); the CISC-macro answer is the other direction on the same axis.

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
This project has three install tiers, from lightest to heaviest. Pick the tier that matches what you need to run; each one is a strict superset of the previous.

Dependency SSOT: pyproject.toml ([project.dependencies] + [project.optional-dependencies]). requirements.txt is a thin convenience mirror for pip install -r requirements.txt; it is kept in sync by hand and must never diverge from pyproject.toml (same versions, same triton pin).

Python requirement: >=3.9 (all tiers).

docs.title: Tier 0: the mininpu sibling package
docs.text:
Everything below depends on the mininpu package: the functional simulator / assembler / ISA SSOT (mininpu.iss, mininpu.asm, mininpu.isa, mininpu.hwparams).

mininpu is not published to PyPI. It lives in a sibling repo and is expected at ../mininpu relative to this repo's checkout:

git clone https://github.com/sunwookim028/mininpu ../mininpu   # if not already cloned
pip install -e ../mininpu

pip install -e . in this repo declares mininpu>=0.4.0 as a dependency, but pip cannot resolve it from PyPI. Install the sibling first, or install this package with --no-deps after mininpu is already present:

pip install -e ../mininpu
pip install -e . --no-deps

docs.title: mininpu runtime subset (internal distribution)
docs.text:
Distribution is internal (a small shared group, not public PyPI). The compiler ships as a self-contained wheel; the mininpu runtime it depends on ships alongside it as a trimmed subset, not the whole mininpu repo.

The shipped mininpu_compiler package has exactly one hard mininpu import: compile.py does from mininpu.asm import assemble_program (the binary-image path). Everything else importing mininpu is tests/demo, not the shipped package.

Verified runtime import closures:

  • import mininpu.asm -> {asm, core, isa}
  • import mininpu.iss.simulator -> {iss, core, isa, hwparams}
  • Neither loads mininpu.cost (its cost imports are lazy, inside functions).
Module Status Reason mininpu/__init__.py REQUIRED package root mininpu/hwparams.py REQUIRED hardware geometry SSOT (sim path) mininpu/isa/ REQUIRED opcode/encoding SSOT mininpu/core/ REQUIRED insn/decode core mininpu/asm/ REQUIRED assembler (assemble_program) mininpu/iss/ REQUIRED functional simulator mininpu/cost/ EXCLUDED not imported by this repo (lazy-only) mininpu/abi.py EXCLUDED not needed by the compiler mininpu/ip_packaging.py EXCLUDED not needed by the compiler everything else (RTL, verification, docs) EXCLUDED not runtime

Two tiers within the subset:

  • compile-only needs {isa, core, asm, hwparams}
  • + functional simulation adds {iss}
  • cost-model features are intentionally unavailable in the subset.

Stage it with:

bash scripts/stage-mininpu-subset.sh
#   MININPU_SRC  source checkout   (default: sibling ../mininpu)
#   DEST         staging dest      (default: <repo>/dist/mininpu-subset)

The script copies only the required subset, runtime-only: it excludes __pycache__/*.pyc and mininpu's internal tests (tests/ dirs and test_*.py files), then runs a static import-closure check. Dropping the tests also removes the only (lazy) mininpu.cost reference, so the artifact has no cost coupling at all. The shared-server destination is TBD; for now it stages into a local dist/ the release process copies onward.

docs.title: Tier 1: sim-only (no compiler build)
docs.text:
Runs the Python functional simulator and unit tests. No MLIR, no C++ toolchain.

pip install -e ../mininpu
pip install numpy pytest      # or: pip install -e .[dev] --no-deps
make sim-test

docs.title: Tier 2: compiler path (npu-opt, npu-translate, e2e tests)
docs.text:
Requires a prebuilt MLIR tree at the pinned LLVM commit.

bash scripts/build-mlir.sh    # clones llvm-project @ pinned commit, ~20 min
make build                    # cmake + ninja: npu-opt, npu-translate, ~1 min
make e2e-test                 # test/e2e/sim_validate (needs npu-opt)

triton-shared-opt (needed for the Triton frontend, tier 3) is built separately:

make build-triton-shared      # ~5 min, after build-mlir

docs.title: Tier 3: Triton frontend (.py kernel -> linalg -> NPU asm)
docs.text:
Only needed for make triton-test (compiling actual Triton kernels, not just running pre-lowered snapshots). Pulls the pinned Triton frontend.

pip install -e .[frontend]    # triton==3.4.0
make triton-test

Triton pin: the only Triton version anywhere in this repo is ==3.4.0 (the [frontend] and [dev] extras in pyproject.toml, mirrored in requirements.txt). triton-shared-opt is built against Triton commit e44bd1c8 == 3.4.0; do not bump one without the other.

docs.title: Optional: demo extra
docs.text:
demo/chat.py and scripts/demo_tinyllama.py load HuggingFace weights and tokenizers. torch is intentionally excluded from this extra: weights load via safetensors directly as numpy; torch is only used for the optional HF reference-output comparison.

pip install -e .[demo]        # transformers, safetensors, tokenizers

The full demo conda-env setup (bash scripts/setup-demo-env.sh) additionally installs CPU-only torch and pre-downloads model checkpoints; see that script for the full package list.

docs.title: Quick reference
docs.text: Goal Command Sim unit tests pip install -e ../mininpu && pip install -e .[dev] --no-deps && make sim-test Compiler e2e tests above + bash scripts/build-mlir.sh && make build && make e2e-test Triton atom-kernel tests above + make build-triton-shared && pip install -e .[frontend] && make triton-test Demo (chat) pip install -e .[demo] (or bash scripts/setup-demo-env.sh for the full conda env)

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text: One-line definitions of every domain-specific term used in this project. For reviewers without MLIR or NPU background; terms appear throughout isa/, sim/, compiler/, and docs/.

docs.title: Hardware model
docs.text: Term Definition NPU Neural Processing Unit - the custom AI accelerator this project targets. DIM Systolic-array tile dimension; DIM=8. All matmul tiles are DIM×DIM. (mininpu/hwparams.py) SPM Scratchpad Memory - bf16 dtype view of the on-chip pool, SPM_ROWS(=2048) × DIM; holds kernel inputs loaded from DRAM. (mininpu/hwparams.py) ACC Accumulator file - fp32 dtype view of the same on-chip pool, ACC_ROWS(=1024) × DIM; receives matmul output and intermediate activations. (mininpu/hwparams.py) VREG Vector Register File - VREG_COUNT(=8) registers × VREG_SIZE(=8) fp32 lanes; holds row-reduced scalars (rowmax, rowsum, etc.). (mininpu/hwparams.py) DRAM External main memory; 64-bit byte-addressed; sparse-backed in the simulator. (mininpu/iss/dram.py) hardware geometry A single fixed profile: DIM=8, one 32 KiB unified on-chip pool (SPM_ROWS=2048 bf16 / ACC_ROWS=1024 fp32 views), VREG_COUNT=8 × VREG_SIZE=8. The former NPU_PROFILE prod/tutorial dual-profile mechanism has been removed; the SSOT is mininpu/hwparams.py. bf16 / fp32 Storage vs accumulator precision. On-chip SPM storage is bf16 (STORAGE_FORMAT in mininpu/hwparams.py); ACC uses fp32 to avoid precision loss during accumulation. systolic array Hardware grid of DIM×DIM MAC units that executes matmul.tile in a single clock cycle. literal pool Assembler section holding wide constants (e.g., DRAM addresses) referenced by instructions as PC-relative offsets. (isa/npu-isa.md, "Why a fixed 64-bit word + literal pool")

docs.title: ISA mechanics
docs.text: Term Definition handle Symbolic name (%foo) for an SPM, ACC, or VREG buffer in .s assembly; the allocator maps it to a physical row/register. (isa/npu-isa.md, "Worked example - physical assembly form") bank Physical storage class: one of spm, acc, vreg. Each has a separate capacity and allocator. (mininpu/asm/_alloc/allocator.py) tile A DIM×DIM (or BLOCK_M×BLOCK_N) unit of work - the granularity at which matmul, DMA, and attention ops execute. opcode 8-bit field in the 64-bit fixed-width ISA instruction selecting the operation. (isa/npu-isa.md, "Why a fixed 64-bit word + literal pool"; mininpu/isa/opcodes.py) macro op CISC-style ISA op that expands to many hardware micro-ops, e.g., matmul.tile drives the systolic array for a full tile. iv / induction variable The loop counter of a loop.begin … loop.end nest; used in DMA address expressions. (isa/npu-isa.md, "Why loop bounds are literal-only (D4)") multi-iv DMA DMA address of the form base + Σᵢ ivᵢ·strideᵢ, supporting tiled DRAM traversal in a single instruction. Deferred to v1.3+ (MultiLinearAddr, D2b); not shipped in the frozen v1.x ISA, which caps iv/iv_stride arity at 1. (isa/npu-isa.md, "Known gaps (not yet implemented)") causal flag matmul.tile attribute that post-masks the upper triangle of the output to −∞ (FlashAttention causal self-attention). accumulate flag matmul.tile attribute that adds into an existing ACC buffer rather than zeroing it first. transB flag matmul.tile attribute that transposes operand B before multiplication (key projection in attention). flush Pipeline fence instruction; required before any host-visible DRAM store to ensure write ordering. (isa/npu-isa.md, "Why flush is the only ordering primitive (D9)") online softmax Algorithm computing softmax incrementally over tiles without materialising the full attention matrix; uses vec.rowmax, vec.exp2, vec.rowsum. (isa/npu-isa.md, "FlashAttention forward - annotated assembly (worked example)") row broadcast Expanding a VREG row-vector (shape [M]) across all columns of a 2D ACC tile (shape [M, N]). col broadcast Expanding a VREG column-vector (shape [N]) across all rows of a 2D ACC tile. Used in RoPE/RMSNorm. iter_arg MLIR scf.for loop-carried value - becomes an in-place handle alias in the NPU translator. (NPUToAsm.cpp, translateOp loop handling)

docs.title: MLIR concepts
docs.text:
These are standard MLIR/LLVM terms; definitions here are purposely simplified for reviewers unfamiliar with the framework.

Term Definition MLIR Multi-Level Intermediate Representation - LLVM's extensible compiler IR framework. All npu-opt/npu-translate transforms operate on MLIR. dialect A namespaced set of ops in MLIR, e.g., npu, linalg, scf, memref. Dialects are registered independently. (compiler/include/NPU/NPU.td) op / Operation A single MLIR instruction with typed operands, attributes, and result values. IR Intermediate Representation - the in-memory (and textual .mlir) program form that passes transform. Pass A self-contained IR transformation registered with a flag, e.g., --linalg-to-npu. (LinalgToNPUPass in LinalgToNPU.cpp) lowering Converting ops in a higher-level dialect to equivalent ops in a lower-level dialect, e.g., linalg → npu. linalg MLIR's linear-algebra dialect: provides linalg.matmul, linalg.reduce, linalg.fill, and the generic linalg.generic. linalg.generic Catch-all op encoding an arbitrary perfectly-nested loop with a body region; triton-shared-opt emits many of these. linalg.matmul Named matmul op C += A @ B (memref operands after bufferisation). Lowered by MatmulLowering. linalg.matmul_transpose_b C += A @ B^T; lowered by MatmulTransposeBLowering. linalg.fill Fills a buffer with a scalar; lowered to vec_zero_acc (zero) or vec_movi (constant). linalg.reduce Reduces along one axis with a body op; maximumf→vec_rowmax, addf→vec_rowsum. scf Structured Control Flow dialect: scf.for (counted loop), scf.yield (terminator). memref A typed reference to a memory region (pointer + shape + layout), analogous to a C array pointer with rank info. bufferization The compiler pass --one-shot-bufferize that converts tensor-valued SSA ops into memref (pointer-based) form. Required before --linalg-to-npu. DPS Destination-Passing Style - linalg ops where the output buffer is an explicit operand (getDpsInits()). OpRewritePattern MLIR base class for pattern-driven IR rewrites: subclasses override matchAndRewrite(). (LinalgToNPU.cpp:97) AffineMap An affine function from loop indices to operand dimensions, used in linalg.generic indexing maps. indexing maps Per-operand AffineMap list on linalg.generic declaring which loop dimensions each operand iterates over. iterator types Per-loop-dimension annotation: parallel (independent iterations) or reduction (must be combined). dyn_cast MLIR/LLVM RTTI downcast - returns nullptr/failure if the type doesn't match. Pervasive in both compiler files. lit / FileCheck LLVM/MLIR unit test tooling: lit runs test files, FileCheck pattern-matches output. FileCheck tests exist under compiler/test/ (Conversion/LinalgToNPU/, Translation/NPUToAsm/) and run via make lit-test. arith MLIR arithmetic dialect: arith.addf, arith.mulf, arith.truncf, arith.extf, arith.select, etc. math MLIR math dialect: math.exp2, math.sqrt, math.rsqrt. TTIR Triton IR - the IR form produced by @triton.jit compilation before lowering to linalg. (mininpu_compiler/compile.py:17)

docs.title: Triton concepts
docs.text: Term Definition Triton Open-source Python-embedded GPU kernel DSL; this project compiles Triton kernels targeting NPU instead of GPU. @triton.jit Decorator marking a Triton kernel; the decorated function is compiled by triton-shared-opt to linalg+scf MLIR. tl.constexpr Compile-time constant kernel parameter; specialised away during kernel compilation (like C++ template args). BLOCK_M / BLOCK_N / BLOCK_K Per-kernel tile dimensions declared as tl.constexpr; must equal DIM (or a multiple) for the NPU backend. pid / program_id Grid coordinate of the current kernel instance - analogous to CUDA's blockIdx. (tl.program_id(axis)) grid N-D launch space; each grid point runs one kernel instance. atom kernel A minimal isolated Triton kernel covering one compute primitive (matmul, softmax row, RMSNorm, etc.). The 9 validated kernels in test/e2e/triton_validate/kernels/ (matmul, fa_fwd_causal_gqa, fa_fwd_noncausal, rmsnorm, rmsnorm_multitile, swiglu, rope, kv_cache, decode_attn) are atom kernels; gptq_matmul is excluded, it bypasses LinalgToNPU via a Builder-based program. triton-shared-opt Third-party MLIR tool (Microsoft/triton-shared) that lowers Triton IR to linalg+scf MLIR. First stage of the NPU pipeline.

docs.title: AI / model terms
docs.text: Term Definition GQA Grouped-Query Attention - H_Q query heads share H_KV key/value heads (H_Q > H_KV); reduces KV-cache size. FA / FlashAttention IO-aware tiled attention algorithm using online softmax to avoid materialising the full N×N attention matrix. RMSNorm Root-Mean-Square LayerNorm: normalises by 1/sqrt(mean(x²)) rather than mean and variance. RoPE Rotary Position Embedding - encodes position by rotating query/key pairs in 2D subspaces. SwiGLU Swish-Gated Linear Unit: silu(gate) * up; used as the FFN activation in LLaMA-2. KV-cache Per-token cached key/value tensors enabling O(1) decode steps in autoregressive generation. residual Skip-connection add in a transformer block; adds layer input to layer output. lm_head Final linear projection from hidden dimension to vocabulary logits; a large GEMM. greedy decode Token-generation strategy that selects the highest-probability token at each step (argmax). prefill Processing the full input prompt in one forward pass, producing the first output token and filling the KV-cache. decode-step Autoregressive generation of one token at a time using the cached KV entries from prefill. TinyLlama-1.1B 22-layer LLaMA-2 variant with 1.1 B parameters; the end-to-end reference model for M4c validation. sm_scale Softmax scaling factor 1/√D applied to attention scores before the online-softmax reduction.

docs.title: Project-specific
docs.text: Term Definition snapshot test Test that starts from a checked-in .mlir file at a specific pipeline stage and validates numerical output; independent of the Triton frontend. (test/e2e/pipeline/) sim-test make sim-test - runs the sim unit-test suite, no compiler binary needed. e2e-test make e2e-test - runs pipeline snapshot + numerical-correctness tests; requires npu-opt + npu-translate. triton-test make triton-test - runs Triton atom-kernel tests through the full pipeline; requires triton-shared-opt too. npu-opt MLIR optimiser tool for the npu dialect, with --linalg-to-npu pass; built to build/compiler/bin/npu-opt. npu-translate MLIR translation tool with --to-asm; emits .s text assembly; built to build/compiler/bin/npu-translate. DRAMAllocator Python allocator managing static (weights) and transient (activations) DRAM address arenas. (mininpu/iss/dram.py) liveness analysis Computes the first and last PC at which each handle is live; drives register allocation. (mininpu/asm/_alloc/liveness.py) first-fit Allocation policy: scan free intervals in order and pick the first one that fits. Used by RowAllocator. linear-scan Register-allocation algorithm: allocate in liveness order, free on last use. Used by both RowAllocator and RegisterAllocator. handle alias When a consumer op can reuse a producer's physical row in-place, the translator maps both handles to the same slot. (NPUToAsm.cpp, handles map in translateOp) SiLU chain Translator side-table tracking a multi-op SwiGLU sequence (sigmoid → negate → exp2 → add1 → div → mul) to fold it into one vec.silu emit. (NPUToAsm.cpp, siluSrcOf) scaleAddOf Translator side-table tracking an RMSNorm reciprocal-scale sequence to fold it into vec.fma.imm. (NPUToAsm.cpp, scaleAddOf) sqrtMarked Translator tag for a math.sqrt result that is about to be divided into 1 - fused into vec.rsqrt. (NPUToAsm.cpp, sqrtMarked)

docs.title: Memory Budget: Qwen 2.5 0.5B GPTQ-Int4 on NPU
docs.text: Target model: Qwen 2.5 0.5B GPTQ-Int4 Target hardware: Custom NPU (geometry SSOT mininpu/hwparams.py), 512 MB DRAM Audience: RTL architect and compiler engineer - exact DRAM / KV-cache sizing for the 512 MB budget. On-chip pool sizing is geometry-dependent and lives with the SSOT (see the geometry pointer at the end), not here.

docs.title: Model config
docs.text:
Model config (confirmed from safetensors header):

Parameter Value hidden_size 896 num_layers 24 num_attention_heads (Q) 14 num_key_value_heads (GQA) 2 (group = 7) head_dim 64 intermediate_size 4864 vocab_size 151,936 group_size (GPTQ) 128 zero_point 7 (uniform) torch_dtype float16

All F16 non-integer tensors confirmed from safetensors (no bf16). Bias on Q [896], K [128], V [128] projections; no bias on O, gate, up, down.

docs.title: DRAM budget (512 MB)
docs.text: This budget is a function of the model weights and KV-cache geometry only; it is independent of the on-chip scratchpad pool.

docs.title: Fixed model weight costs
docs.text:
All weights are in the reformatted NPU layout: [N, K//2] uint8 for quantized weights, [N_groups, N] F16 for scales, [N] F16 for biases.

Per-layer weight breakdown:

Projection Shape (K, N) int4 bytes [N, K//2] Scales [groups, N] F16 Bias [N] F16 Total q_proj K=896, N=896 896×448 = 401 KB 7×896×2 = 12 KB 896×2 = 2 KB 415 KB k_proj K=896, N=128 128×448 = 56 KB 7×128×2 = 2 KB 128×2 = 0.3 KB 58 KB v_proj K=896, N=128 56 KB 2 KB 0.3 KB 58 KB o_proj K=896, N=896 401 KB 12 KB - 413 KB gate_proj K=896, N=4864 4864×448 = 2129 KB 7×4864×2 = 66 KB - 2195 KB up_proj K=896, N=4864 2129 KB 66 KB - 2195 KB down_proj K=4864, N=896 896×2432 = 2129 KB 38×896×2 = 66 KB - 2195 KB input_layernorm [896] F16 - - 2 KB 2 KB post_attn_layernorm [896] F16 - - 2 KB 2 KB Per-layer total 7,533 KB = 7.35 MB

Full model:

Component Size Notes 24 × layer weights 176.1 MB All projections + norms embed_tokens / lm_head (tied) 259.7 MB [151936, 896] F16; single storage model.norm.weight 1.8 KB Final layernorm Fixed total 435.8 MB

docs.title: Available for KV cache
docs.text:
512 MB total
- 435.8 MB fixed weights
= 76.2 MB available
- ~1.0 MB reserved for DRAM activation buffers and runtime scratch
  (hidden state x: 32×896×2 ≈ 56 KB; input/residual copy: ≈56 KB;
   FFN intermediate if spilled: 32×4864×2 ≈ 304 KB;
   runtime overhead stack/descriptors: ~256 KB → total ≈700 KB, rounded up to 1 MB)
= 75.2 MB for KV cache

docs.title: KV cache per token
docs.text:
Each token appends one K vector and one V vector per layer, per KV head:

KV per token = 24 layers × 2 (K+V) × 2 KV-heads × 64 head_dim × 2 bytes (fp16)
             = 24 × 2 × 2 × 64 × 2
             = 12,288 bytes = 12 KB per token

docs.title: Maximum context budget (512 MB DRAM, fp16 KV)
docs.text:
Max context tokens = floor(75.2 MB / 12 KB) = floor(75.2×1024×1024 / 12288) = 6,417 tokens

Context / output generation budget:

Input tokens Max generated tokens Total context 512 5,905 6,417 1,024 5,393 6,417 2,048 4,369 6,417 4,096 2,321 6,417 6,000 417 6,417

Note: Qwen 2.5's max_position_embeddings = 32,768, but 512 MB DRAM limits the hardware context to 6,417 tokens at fp16 KV precision (after reserving 1 MB for DRAM activation buffers).

docs.title: Context extension options
docs.text:
KV dtype Bytes/token Max context Notes fp16 (default) 12,288 6,417 No quality loss int8 quantized 6,144 12,834 ~0.5% perplexity increase int4 quantized 3,072 25,668 ~1-2% perplexity increase

All figures use 75.2 MB KV budget (76.2 MB − 1 MB activation overhead). Formulae: floor(75.2×1024×1024 / bytes_per_token).

For int8/int4 KV, additional dequant ops are needed during attention (KV fetch → dequant → fp16 for matmul). The NPU ISA already has dma.load.spm.i8 and vec.dequant.i4 which can be extended for this purpose.

docs.title: Hardware geometry
docs.text: Hardware geometry: see the SSOT mininpu/hwparams.py (DIM=8, one 32 KiB unified pool).

docs.title: Model / precision facts (recovered from the removed ARCH_SPEC_NPU.md)
docs.text:
docs/ARCH_SPEC_NPU.md was deleted and this file was trimmed; the following precision-contract facts for the Qwen 2.5 0.5B target survived only in working memory and are restored here. The model config already documented above (hidden=896, 24 layers, 14 Q : 2 KV group-7 heads, head_dim=64, inter=4864, vocab=151936, group_size=128, zero_point=7, float16, bias-on-QKV) is unchanged.

Positional / norm constants (host-side): - rope_theta = 1_000_000 (NOT 10_000) - host-side cos/sin table parameter. - rms_norm_eps = 1e-6. - tie_word_embeddings = True - embedding and lm_head share one weight, stored once.

GPTQ host-side offline repack (done before DMA, not on-NPU): - Weights: on-disk [K//8, N] int32 (8 nibbles per int32) -> repacked to [N, K//2] uint8 for NPU DMA. - Zeros: qzeros [N_groups, N//8] int32 -> unpacked to [N_groups, N] fp16 (all values = 7).

Accumulation precision: - fp32 accumulation is MANDATORY for the large contraction dims (K=896, K=4864). fp16 accumulation error at K=4864 is ~5x the fp32 path.

Op-lowering notes: - Bias-add is implemented as dma.load.spm(dram_stride=0) broadcast + cast.f16.f32 + vec.add.acc. - lm_head reuses matmul.tile - no new opcode is introduced for it.

For how these lower through the backend see COMPILER_ARCHITECTURE.md; numeric tolerance contracts are the sibling ISA's § Numerical Precision Contracts in ../isa/npu-isa.md.

docs.title: Quality Audit: mininpu-compiler
docs.text: Scope: the compiler-side repo only (Triton to custom NPU ISA, MLIR based). The sibling mininpu ISS/simulator package and its hwparams.py/opcodes.py SSOTs are out of scope and are referenced only as the authority the compiler must agree with.

docs.title: Executive summary
docs.text:
• The compiler tree has a genuinely clean, recognizable out-of-tree MLIR skeleton: TableGen npu dialect, include/lib/tools layering, npu-opt as an MlirOptMain driver, npu-translate as an MlirTranslateMain hook, idiomatic add_mlir_library targets, and an excellent "why" comment culture (W9/D2b/D4 rationale).
  • Two issues rise above the rest and should be fixed first:
  • CI is broken: the e2e-tests job calls a nonexistent make triton-test-tutorial target (blocker), and both jobs pip install a mininpu dependency that is not resolvable on a stock runner (high).
  • A latent correctness hazard: PhysAllocPass::getPhysProfile() still implements the removed NPU_PROFILE mechanism and defaults to DIM=16 / VREG_COUNT=32, contradicting the DIM=8 / VREG_COUNT=8 hardware SSOT, so the allocator can hand out physically-invalid VREG indices.
  • The strongest "not yet a real MLIR project" signals are structural, not buggy: no lit infrastructure (a hand-rolled bash FileCheck runner stands in and is not run in CI), and real compiler work (physical allocation, linear-address analysis) living inside the translation library instead of as npu-opt passes, producing a ~1494-line translator with self-admitted parallel dispatch chains.
  • The dialect is small and readable, but its defining invariant (three memory banks: ACC = rank-2 f32, SPM = rank-2 f16, VREG = rank-1 f32) is enforced by hand-written C++ verifiers on only 5 of 26 ops while every operand is the loose AnyMemRef. This is the top MLIR-idiom improvement available.
  • Documentation is well organized with real SSOT discipline, but has a verifiable staleness cluster around decode-step attention (shipped and tested, still documented as unimplemented/planned in two files), a fully fictional README demo/chat.py section, and several stale GLOSSARY.md cross-references and counts.
  • The bulk of the remaining backlog is low-risk hygiene, doc accuracy, and safe code cleanups that can be auto-applied.

docs.title: Per-dimension health
docs.text: Dimension Health Notes Repository hygiene Good .gitignore thorough; no build/pycache artifacts tracked; two real gaps: tracked dangling symlink, missing LICENSE. Build / CI infra Needs work CI references a missing Make target (blocker) and an unresolvable mininpu dep (high); requirements/pyproject drift; host-specific fallback paths. MLIR dialect (NPU.td) Fair Clean and readable, but bank invariants live in C++ verifiers on 5/26 ops; loose AnyMemRef; coarse op-level effects; dead causal attr. MLIR conversion (LinalgToNPU) Healthy No findings surfaced beyond the shared translation/layering concerns; conversion split is idiomatic. MLIR translation (NPUToAsm) Needs work ~1100-line translateOp; dead NPU_PROFILE geometry (correctness hazard); parallel dispatch chains with divergent isDRAM; inconsistent immediate formatting; residual silent-drop paths. Python driver + tests Fair Driver well documented; boilerplate duplicated across e2e tests; no unit coverage of driver logic; no subprocess timeouts; inconsistent test-package layout. Docs Fair Strong SSOT discipline; decode-attn staleness cluster; fictional README demo section; several stale GLOSSARY xrefs/counts. MLIR expert lens Needs work No lit; allocation/addr-analysis inside translation not as passes; dead geometry contradicts SSOT; no Passes.td/generated docs; no negative FileCheck coverage.

docs.text: file:line Issue Recommendation fix_class effort .github/workflows/ci.yml:29 Both jobs pip install -r requirements.txt, which pins mininpu>=0.4.0 not on PyPI, with no index/git source configured. Unresolvable on a stock runner. Check out the sibling mininpu and pip install -e <path>, or add a git+https://...@<tag> source, or publish wheels reachable by CI. semantic-defer M PhysAllocPass.cpp:52 (+ NPUToAsm.cpp:66-67) getPhysProfile() keeps the removed NPU_PROFILE mechanism; default {dim=16, vreg=32} contradicts the DIM=8 / VREG=8 SSOT. Allocator can emit VREG indices the frozen ISA cannot encode. Correctness hazard, not just stale text. Delete getPhysProfile()/PhysProfile and the env lookup; source DIM/SPM_ROWS/ACC_ROWS/VREG_COUNT from the hwparams SSOT (generated header or hardcoded DIM=8 with a pointer comment). Fix the arch-header note. semantic-defer M NPU.td:36 All 26 ops use loose AnyMemRef; the dialect-defining three-bank constraint lives in hand-written C++ verifiers wired to only 5 ops. Least idiomatic ODS pattern. Define NPU_AccMemRef/NPU_SpmMemRef/NPU_VregMemRef TypeConstraints and use them in arguments; add AllShapesMatch on elementwise binaries; delete the per-bank C++ checks. semantic-defer L NPUToAsm.cpp:255 translateOp is a single ~1100-line function; the linalg.generic body matcher (710-1073) is a ~360-line nested if/else mixing SiLU fold, RMSNorm fold, broadcast, causal elision, general elementwise. Decompose by op family into free functions over (Operation, TranslatorState); keep translateOp a thin dispatcher; makes fold helpers unit-testable. semantic-defer L scripts/run-filecheck-tests.sh:1 FileCheck tests use a hand-rolled bash runner (only %s, pipes RUN lines to /dev/null, discards diagnostics) and are not run in CI. docs.title: Deployment posture (clarified 2026-07-08)
docs.text:
The ci.yml:29 finding above and the "published wheels" option in the deferred appendix were written assuming a public-PyPI target. That is not the actual deployment model, and the finding's severity/framing should be read accordingly:

  • Distribution is internal only: a minimal group of about 50 users, via an internal/shared-server artifact (a self-contained linux_x86_64 wheel or the bundled binaries), not public PyPI.
  • The sibling mininpu runtime is likewise not published to PyPI; only the compiler-needed subset is shared internally alongside this package.
  • Because of this, manylinux/PyPI-tag honesty work is not a project goal, and the mininpu resolution gap in the public GitHub Actions CI job (which cannot reach an internal source on a bare runner) is a deprioritized optional mirror, not a release blocker. The internal artifact is smoke-tested against mininpu installed from source (see scripts/smoke-wheel.sh), which is the path that matters for real distribution.
  • Concrete mininpu subset decided: the compiler ships only the runtime it needs (isa, core, asm, iss, hwparams), excluding cost/, abi.py, and ip_packaging.py. scripts/stage-mininpu-subset.sh operationalizes this (verified import closure; local dist/ staging today, shared-server path TBD).

docs.title: Medium
docs.text: file:line Issue Recommendation fix_class effort NPUToAsm.cpp:46 linalg.generic semantics re-matched in the translator via an ad-hoc second body matcher parallel to LinalgToNPU (layering violation; adding an op needs synced edits in two dyn_cast chains). Move SiLU/RMSNorm/causal-mask matchers into LinalgToNPU patterns; make a surviving tensor-body linalg.generic a hard pass error; collapse the two dispatch chains into one visitor. semantic-defer L NPUToAsm.cpp:28 Physical allocation and linear-addr analysis run as free functions inside the --to-asm hook, yielding a 1494-line translator; allocation result cannot be FileCheck'd as MLIR. Promote PhysAllocPass/LinearAddrAnalysis to real OperationPass<func::FuncOp> passes that annotate ops (spm_row/acc_row/vreg/addr); reduce NPUToAsm to a printer. semantic-defer L PhysAllocPass.cpp:220 Two parallel dyn_cast chains (walkOp vs translateOp) must be hand-synced; their isDRAM helpers already diverged (translator unwraps ReinterpretCast+SubView, alloc unwraps only SubView). Hoist one shared isDRAM into NPUAsmInternal.h; longer term drive liveness and emission from a single op description. semantic-defer M NPU.td:28 Coarse op-level MemoryEffects<[MemRead, MemWrite]> loses per-operand read/write dataflow (matmul reads A/B, RMW C; vec_add_acc reads acc_a/acc_b, writes acc_dst). Attach effects to operands via Arg<..., [MemRead]>/[MemWrite]; drop the op-level trait. semantic-defer M NPU.td:41 MatmulTileOp causal attribute is undocumented and never set by lowering; mm.getCausal() is always false. Real causal state lives in a separate causalMatmuls side-table. Pick one SSOT: wire causal through the attribute and retire the side-table (and document it), or remove the dead attribute. semantic-defer M NPUDialect.cpp:22 Only 5 of 26 ops set hasVerifier=1; 21 ops accept malformed operands despite documented bank invariants. After ODS type constraints land, add hasVerifier=1 + small shared checks only where cross-operand shape relations exist (broadcast, row-reduce, fma). code-safe M NPUToAsm.cpp:998 Residual silent-drop paths (linalg.reduce unknown body 519-522, unsupported divf 998, 2D maxnumf 1055) contradict the stated "zero silent no-ops" W9 invariant. Route these through emitOpError + translationFailed, or document each as a legitimate no-emit alias. code-safe S NPUToAsm.cpp:432 Immediates formatted inconsistently across emitters (hardcoded imm=1.000000e+00 vs default stream imm=1 vs to_string), making .s output non-uniform and fragile. Introduce one formatImm(double) helper and a uniform operand/kwarg builder; use everywhere. code-safe M test/e2e/sim_validate/test_gemm_e2e.py:14 Compiler-locate/skip/subprocess boilerplate copy-pasted across 4 e2e test files instead of conftest.py. Move skip_no_compiler and a shared run_compiler(mlir, asm) into conftest.py (or test/e2e/_pipeline_helpers.py); import in all four. code-safe M mininpu_compiler/compile.py:260 Pipeline subprocess.run calls have no timeout=; a hung tool wedges the whole test/CI run with no diagnostic. Add a generous timeout= to each pipeline call and raise RuntimeError('<tool> timed out after Ns') on TimeoutExpired. code-safe S mininpu_compiler/compile.py:46 No unit tests exercise the driver's branchy logic (_find_tool precedence, _newer_libstdcpp_dir, compile cache, CLI parsing); only reached transitively by skipped e2e tests. Add test/unit/test_compile_driver.py with monkeypatch/tmp fakes covering tool resolution order, cache hit/miss/clear, and _cli_main arg parsing. semantic-defer M .antigravitycli/4794d343-...json Tracked file is a symlink (git mode 120000) to ~/.gemini/... outside the repo; dangling here, would leak another tool's local config elsewhere. Introduced incidentally. git rm it and add .antigravitycli/ to .gitignore. hygiene S pyproject.toml:12 license = {text = "MIT"} but no LICENSE/COPYING file exists; a common packaging/legal blocker for PyPI distribution. Add an MIT LICENSE file at repo root. hygiene S requirements.txt:1 Duplicates and drifts from pyproject deps (triton>=3.0 vs pinned ==3.4.0); adds demo deps with no pyproject extra, so .[dev] alone gives no working demo. Make pyproject the sole SSOT: add a demo extra; delete requirements.txt or reduce to -e .[dev,demo]; drop the divergent triton pin. hygiene S compiler/include/NPU/CMakeLists.txt:1 No Passes.td/-gen-pass-decls and no add_mlir_doc (-gen-op-doc/-gen-dialect-doc); passes hand-registered, op catalog maintained by hand in isa/npu-isa.md. Add Passes.td with -gen-pass-decls and a registerNPUPasses() header; add add_mlir_doc targets so the op reference is generated from NPU.td. semantic-defer M compiler/test/Conversion/LinalgToNPU/linalgtonpu.mlir:1 No negative/verifier FileCheck coverage despite four ops declaring hasVerifier=1; zero -verify-diagnostics/not cases; only happy-path mnemonic assertions. Once lit is wired, add -verify-diagnostics cases per verifier and not npu-translate cases for documented hard errors (phys-alloc overflow, unresolved DRAM addr, unmatched body). semantic-defer M docs/SUPPORTED_TRITON.md:197 Section 5 says N=1 decode-step attention is unimplemented ("decode loop runs in numpy"); it actually ships and is tested (decode_attn.py, test_decode_attn). Contradicts README. Remove the row from section 5; add decode_attn to the validated list in section 1. docs S isa/npu-isa.md:283 Lines 283-284 (Planned) and 311-312 (Known gaps #11) describe decode-step attention as future work; it already ships and is validated. Move the bullet to Completed; update/remove known-gap #11. docs S README.md:204 Lines 204-281 document demo/chat.py / python -m demo.chat as a working feature, but no demo/ dir exists; the Makefile itself flags it as a placeholder. Gate the whole section behind a "planned / not yet landed" banner matching the Makefile, or move it to a roadmap doc until the file lands. docs S docs/charter.md:48 References GitHub issue #120, which does not exist; the actual tracking issue is #11 (dialect<->ISA drift test). Fix the reference to #11. docs S GLOSSARY.md:91 Atom-kernel count disagrees across docs (7 vs 7 vs 8 vs actual 9-10 files); "multi-iv DMA" entry (line 35) describes a v1.3+ deferred, not-shipped feature as present-tense. Recount kernels and use one number in README/SUPPORTED_TRITON/GLOSSARY/isa; note gptq_matmul is excluded (bypasses LinalgToNPU); add a "deferred to v1.3+" caveat to multi-iv DMA. docs M GLOSSARY.md:21 Cites isa/npu-isa.md sections §1/§3/§5/§7/§8/§8.3 that do not exist (that file has no numbered sections). Replace the §N citations with the actual Markdown header text, or add numbered headers to isa/npu-isa.md. docs S GLOSSARY.md:74 Claims lit/FileCheck is "not yet used, tracked in #10"; both false: FileCheck tests exist under compiler/test/ and run via make lit-test, and #10 is an unrelated lowering issue. Update the entry to say FileCheck tests exist and run via make lit-test; drop the #10 pointer. docs S

docs.title: Deferred / needs-owner-decision appendix
docs.text:
These require judgement, cross-repo coordination, or architectural change and should NOT be auto-applied:

  • CI mininpu dependency resolution (ci.yml:29): needs a decision on how the sibling repo is made installable in the public GitHub Actions CI job (checkout + editable install, or an internal git/index source). Not a public wheel: see "Deployment posture" above. Deprioritized: internal distribution does not depend on this CI job. Cross-repo.
  • Geometry SSOT reconciliation (PhysAllocPass.cpp:52, NPUToAsm.cpp:66-67, PhysAllocPass.cpp:154): removing NPU_PROFILE and sourcing DIM/SPM_ROWS/ACC_ROWS/ VREG_COUNT from hwparams.py (ideally a generated header) changes emitted allocation behavior; verify against the frozen ISA/sim before landing.
  • translateOp decomposition (NPUToAsm.cpp:255) and hoisting allocation/addr-analysis into real npu-opt passes (NPUToAsm.cpp:28, PhysAllocPass.cpp:220): large refactors that alter the pass/translation boundary; land incrementally with FileCheck coverage.
  • Dialect type-constraint rework (NPU.td:36), per-operand memory effects (NPU.td:28), and the causal-attribute SSOT decision (NPU.td:41): behavioral ODS changes; sequence after they unblock the hasVerifier coverage work.
  • lit adoption (run-filecheck-tests.sh:1) + negative FileCheck coverage (linalgtonpu.mlir:1) + Passes.td/generated docs (CMakeLists.txt:1): the "real MLIR project" structural upgrade; best done as one coordinated change.
  • linalg.generic matcher relocation into LinalgToNPU (NPUToAsm.cpp:46): resolves the layering leak but changes where lowering failures surface; needs owner sign-off.
  • Driver unit-test suite (compile.py:46) and the profile param rename/removal (compile.py:321): low risk individually but touch public-ish CLI/test surface.

docs.title: Appendix: Improvement pass applied (2026-07-08)
docs.text:
The audit findings were acted on in four commits on branch chore/quality-audit-improvements (landed off master, not merged):

  • docs: doc-accuracy pass + MIT LICENSE + CONTRIBUTING.md (decode_attn shipped-state fixes, atom-kernel count reconciled to 9, GLOSSARY citations, gated unlanded README demo).
  • build: dependency SSOT in pyproject (unified triton==3.4.0, demo extra), removed tracked .antigravitycli session artifact, folded pytest config into pyproject (deleted pytest.ini), Makefile FileCheck path, activate.sh de-hardcoding, dropped stale CI step, new docs/DEPENDENCIES.md.
  • refactor (python): compile.py _build_ttir_module dedup, subprocess timeouts, CLI RuntimeError handling; test package __init__ + removed redundant sys.path inserts; shared conftest skip_no_compiler/run_compiler helpers.
  • refactor (C++/MLIR): NPU.td dialect description + unused include drop, verifyRowReduce dedup, formatImm/aliasHandle helpers, dead loop-bound removal, deprecated PointerUnion is<>/get<> -> isa<>/cast<>, named default-dim constant flagging the DIM=8 SSOT reconciliation.

Verification in this environment (Ubuntu 24.04, 32 cores): - MLIR toolchain built from the pinned LLVM commit; make build compiles npu-opt and npu-translate cleanly (exit 0). - make lit-test: 2 passed, 0 failed (FileCheck golden output for the LinalgToNPU and NPUToAsm tests is byte-identical, confirming the C++ refactors preserved emitted assembly). - make e2e-test: 6 passed, 6 failed, 30 skipped. All 6 failures are pre-existing and originate in the sibling mininpu package (mininpu/asm/_alloc/liveness.py raising "use of 'rN' without prior def"), not in this repo's compiler. They are unchanged by this branch. - Python test collection is identical before and after (209 tests); no new failures introduced.

docs.title: Top deferred items (need owner decision, not auto-applied)
docs.text:
1. Sibling mininpu skew blocks the e2e/sim suite (liveness rejects currently-emitted assembly). This is the single biggest blocker to a green test run and must be resolved in the mininpu repo (or the compiler's emitted handle/def ordering reconciled with the new liveness contract).
  2. NPU_PROFILE remnant in PhysAllocPass defaults dim to 16 while the hardware SSOT is DIM=8: source geometry from hwparams and delete getPhysProfile/PhysProfile.
  3. Adopt real lit (lit.cfg.py + llvm-lit) and retire the hand-rolled bash FileCheck runner; wire into CI.
  4. Decompose the 1500-line NPUToAsm translator into per-op-family emitters; consider promoting PhysAllocPass/LinearAddrAnalysis to attribute-annotating passes.
  5. Tighten the dialect: NPU-specific memref TypeConstraints in ODS replacing loose AnyMemRef; move body-pattern matchers (SiLU/RMSNorm/causal-mask) from the translator into LinalgToNPU patterns.

docs.title: Supported Triton Constructs
docs.text:
SSOT - compile surface. This file is the authoritative record of what the NPU compiler can and cannot lower from Triton. Peer to charter.md (roadmap/design SSOT) and the sibling mininpu/isa/opcodes.py (ISA SSOT). Defects/gaps are tracked as GitHub issues, not here.

What the NPU compiler can and cannot lower from Triton source. Use this as a pre-flight checklist before writing a new kernel.

docs.title: 1. Supported tl.* primitives
docs.text:
These primitives are empirically validated through the 9 atom kernels in test/e2e/triton_validate/kernels/ (matmul, fa_fwd_causal_gqa, fa_fwd_noncausal, rmsnorm, rmsnorm_multitile, swiglu, rope, kv_cache, decode_attn; gptq_matmul is excluded, it bypasses LinalgToNPU via a Builder-based program). Any usage pattern found in those kernels compiles and produces correct output; other usage patterns are untested.

Primitive Notes Validated by tl.program_id(axis) Grid coordinate; axes 0, 1, 2 all work all kernels tl.arange(0, N) Constant range; N must be tl.constexpr all kernels tl.constexpr parameters Compile-time specialisation all kernels tl.load(ptr, mask, other) mask/other supported; cache_modifier ignored all kernels tl.store(ptr, val, mask) mask supported all kernels tl.zeros((M, N), dtype) Initialises ACC tile matmul, fa, rmsnorm_mt tl.full((M, N), val, dtype) Constant fill fa tl.dot(a, b, acc=…) Both acc and non-acc forms matmul, fa tl.trans(a) Transposes a 2D block (key projection) fa tl.max(x, axis=1) Row-reduce max fa tl.sum(x, axis=1) Row-reduce sum fa, rmsnorm, rmsnorm_mt tl.maximum(a, b) Element-wise max (online-softmax update) fa tl.where(cond, x, y) Only the FA causal-mask pattern compiles (see §4) fa tl.math.exp2(x) Element-wise exp₂ fa, swiglu tl.sqrt(x) Element-wise sqrt (via math.sqrt in linalg) rmsnorm, rmsnorm_mt .to(tl.float16/.float32) Type cast (truncf / extf) all kernels

Untested / known not to compile: tl.exp, tl.log, tl.minimum, tl.softmax, tl.reshape, tl.broadcast_to, tl.cat, tl.atomic_*, tl.cdiv, tl.dot without acc= and with non-constexpr K, tl.where outside the FA causal-mask pattern.

docs.title: 2. LinalgToNPU rewrite patterns
docs.text: These 21 patterns in compiler/lib/Conversion/LinalgToNPU/LinalgToNPU.cpp convert named linalg ops and specific linalg.generic body shapes to npu.* dialect ops. Every op matched here will be correctly lowered to assembly.

docs.title: Matmul (M3)
docs.text:
Pattern linalg op npu op File:line MatmulLowering linalg.matmul (C += A @ B) npu.matmul_tile :97 MatmulTransposeBLowering linalg.matmul_transpose_b npu.matmul_tile{transB} :118

accumulate flag is set when C is defined by memref.alloc (on-chip ACC), not when it is a function argument (DRAM). The distinction is purely structural.

docs.title: Fill / initialise (M3)
docs.text: Pattern linalg op npu op File:line FillLowering linalg.fill 2D f32 with zero npu.vec_zero_acc :138 FillLowering linalg.fill 1D f32 with constant npu.vec_movi :138

docs.title: Reductions ACC→VREG (M3)
docs.text:
Pattern linalg op npu op File:line ReduceMaxLowering linalg.reduce{maximumf} 2D→1D npu.vec_rowmax :175 ReduceSumLowering linalg.reduce{addf} 2D→1D npu.vec_rowsum :206

Only maximumf and addf reduction bodies are matched. minimumf, mulf, and other reducers fall through to the ad-hoc matcher (§3) or emit nothing.

docs.title: FlashAttention vector ops (M3) - ACC ↔ VREG
docs.text:
These patterns lower the element-wise tile ops used in online-softmax.

Pattern linalg.generic body npu op File:line VecMaximumLowering 1D×1D→1D maximumf npu.vec_maximum :235 SubBcastLowering 2D − 1D(row) → 2D subf npu.vec_sub_bcast :260 Exp2AccLowering 2D → 2D math.exp2 npu.vec_exp2 :285 Exp2VregLowering 1D → 1D math.exp2 npu.vec_exp2_vreg :312 ScaleBcastLowering 2D × 1D(row) → 2D mulf npu.vec_scale_bcast :335 DivBcastLowering 2D / 1D(row) → 2D divf npu.vec_div_bcast :363 SubVregLowering 1D − 1D → 1D subf npu.vec_sub_vreg :417 FmaVregLowering 3×1D → 1D mulf+addf (FMA) npu.vec_fma :443 CopyVregLowering memref.copy 1D f32 npu.vec_copy_vreg :481

docs.title: Casts (M3 + M4a)
docs.text: Pattern linalg.generic body npu op File:line TruncF32F16Lowering 2D → 2D arith.truncf f32→f16 npu.cast_f32_f16 :388 ExtendF16F32Lowering 2D → 2D arith.extf f16→f32 npu.cast_f16_f32 :504

docs.title: Element-wise ACC ops (M4a - RMSNorm, SwiGLU, RoPE)
docs.text:
Pattern linalg.generic body npu op File:line MulAccLowering 2D × 2D → 2D mulf npu.vec_mul_acc :532 AddAccLowering 2D + 2D → 2D addf npu.vec_add_acc :557 SubAccLowering 2D − 2D → 2D subf npu.vec_sub_acc :582 ScaleColBcastLowering 2D × 1D(col) → 2D mulf npu.vec_scale_col_bcast :607 RsqrtVregLowering 1D → 1D math.rsqrt npu.vec_rsqrt :636

Note: SiluAccLowering is declared (:28) but not added to the pipeline (:657-660). The SwiGLU silu folding is handled ad-hoc in the translator (§3). This is tracked in #10.

This was the historical entry-point before LinalgToNPU grew its pattern set; some patterns here predate their dialect equivalents. The split is tracked in

Op Lowered to Where linalg.matmul npu.matmul_tile LinalgToNPU.cpp:97 linalg.matmul_transpose_b npu.matmul_tile{transB} LinalgToNPU.cpp:118 linalg.fill (2D f32 zero) npu.vec_zero_acc LinalgToNPU.cpp:138 linalg.fill (1D f32 const) npu.vec_movi LinalgToNPU.cpp:138 linalg.reduce{maximumf} 2D→1D npu.vec_rowmax LinalgToNPU.cpp:175 linalg.reduce{addf} 2D→1D npu.vec_rowsum LinalgToNPU.cpp:206 memref.copy 1D f32 npu.vec_copy_vreg LinalgToNPU.cpp:481

docs.title: mininpu-compiler - Charter
docs.text: The single source of truth for this project's milestone ladder and its key design decisions. Roadmap-style planning lives here; current defects/gaps are tracked as GitHub issues; the compile surface lives in SUPPORTED_TRITON.md.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: A minimal, extensible compiler from Triton to a custom NPU ISA. The ISA is deliberately small - target band 20-50 instructions (still being defined; see the SSOT below) - and every workload must be expressible as tiled loops over DIM×DIM matmul + vector primitives. The compile target is transformer inference kernels: GPT-2-style blocks (LayerNorm, GELU, standard MHA) and the LLaMA family (RMSNorm, RoPE, SwiGLU, GQA/FlashAttention), composed into end-to-end decode.

docs.title: Milestone versions
docs.text: Two independent version axes - do not conflate them. This milestone version (v0/v1/v2/…) tracks compiler capability. It is distinct from the ISA version axis (the sibling package's ISA_VERSION, currently 0.4.0, and the spec-narrative "v1.x freeze" codename). A milestone vN may ride on any ISA_VERSION.

docs.title: v0 - DONE (baseline)
docs.text: Triton → NPU end-to-end for tiled GEMM, FlashAttention forward, and Qwen 2.5 0.5B greedy decode. Folds the historical milestones M0-M5c (see appendix). The current tree is on the single-geometry re-sync (#9): one fixed DIM=8 geometry, no NPU_PROFILE dual profile. Full end-to-end re-verification is currently gated on the sibling ISA settling (ISA_VERSION is in flux and mininpu.asm does not import cleanly today), so v0 is "done by construction, pending a green re-run". The sibling mininpu package is under active development right now (ISA rework in progress); sibling-coupled actions - the mininpu dependency-pin finalization, end-to-end re-verification, and pipeline-snapshot regeneration - are deliberately deferred until the sibling ISA settles. The mininpu>=0.4.0 pin in pyproject.toml is therefore provisional.

docs.title: v1 - NOW: GPT-2 op coverage + compiler-architecture prework
docs.text:
• Op coverage: LayerNorm, GELU, residual-add, standard (non-GQA) MHA. The SSOT already reserves vec.gelu; the dialect does not yet emit it (tracked in #11).
  • Compiler prework this requires:
  • A real multi-op elementwise-body lowering strategy (retire the ad-hoc translator side-tables; land SiluAccLowering and friends in LinalgToNPU).
  • Restore the ISA-coverage drift test (currently xfail'd, see #11).
  • Consider generating the two mirrored dyn_cast chains (PhysAllocPass ↔ translateOps) from NPU.td instead of hand-syncing them.

Details and acceptance criteria live in the v1-labeled GitHub tracking issues (umbrella #21).

docs.title: v2 and beyond - TBD
docs.text: Scope not yet defined. Candidate directions (unscheduled, not committed to a version): FPGA/ZCU102 deployment, FP8 execution path (Phase 2 ISS handler / Phase 3 driver), broader model-family kernels.

docs.title: Design decisions (SSOT)
docs.text:
• ISA SSOT = sibling mininpu/mininpu/isa/opcodes.py (the frozen opcode / field / flag / capacity table + precision contracts). Do not hard-code the opcode count in docs - read opcodes.py (currently 38 opcodes, RISC-style; target band 20-50). isa/npu-isa.md is non-normative narrative. A CISC-style variant is under active consideration - the project may migrate to CISC or evaluate RISC and CISC side by side - which would reshape the compiler's lowering granularity (relevant to v1 prework); this is an open design direction, not a decision.
  • Hardware-geometry SSOT = sibling mininpu/mininpu/hwparams.py: a single fixed geometry, DIM=8, one 32 KiB unified on-chip pool (SPM/ACC are dtype views of the same byte-pool, not separate budgets). No NPU_PROFILE.
  • Tiling model: scf.for → loop.begin/loop.end + matmul.tile; loop bounds are literal-only and patched host-side (per-pid via the ABI patch table); all shapes are tile-aligned (host pads partial tiles).
  • Milestone-tracking policy: this charter defines the ladder; execution is tracked in GitHub issues labeled v0/v1/v2/…. This file replaces the retired roadmap doc.

docs.title: Appendix - historical milestones (M0-M5c, all folded into v0)
docs.text: # Deliverable M0 ISA spec + functional-sim skeleton M1 Sim runs hand-authored FlashAttention M2 MLIR npu dialect, npu-opt, npu-translate; GEMM end-to-end M3 Full FA-forward lowering from linalg+scf M4a ISA v1.1 (7 new ops), dual profiles, Triton atom kernels + test harness M4b LLaMA-2 transformer block (KV-cache append, multi-tile-D RMSNorm, orchestrator); 7/7 Triton atoms M4c TinyLlama-1.1B multi-layer greedy decode; HF weight bridge; token-exact vs HuggingFace M5a ISA v2 + ABI v2 (9 new opcodes: .mi DMA family, loop.begin.csr, barrier); sim handlers + 8 ISS Qwen decode tests M5b mininpu-compiler package; all Triton atoms wired for Qwen 2.5 0.5B; TRITON_INTERPRET=1 token match vs numpy M5c ISS full decode coverage (RMSNorm, SwiGLU, RoPE, GQA attention through ISS dispatch)

docs.title: Efficiency (design notes) - why v2 exists
docs.text:
The v0/v1 work above establishes that the target kernels (arbitrary-size tiled GEMM, FlashAttention forward, Qwen decode) are expressible. They are not yet efficient. v2's reason to exist is closing that gap; the FPGA/FP8/model-family candidates listed under v2 are downstream of it. See COMPILER_ARCHITECTURE.md §5 for where these live in the backend.

Four structural gaps:

  1. No instruction scheduler / no double-buffering. Serialization (NPUToAsm.cpp) is a naive in-order IR-walk: no reordering, no software pipelining, DMA and compute strictly sequential.
  2. The 32 KiB unified pool is not reused across kernels - no cross-kernel residency planning, so operands are re-DMA'd.
  3. Non-matmul (vector/elementwise) ops are under-served by the pipeline relative to the matmul path.
  4. DIM=8 systolic array is small (hardware ceiling, not compiler work).

Levers:

  • A scheduling + async-DMA pass that actually exploits flags[5]=async (today a functional no-op) to overlap DMA under compute and enable double-buffering. Tracked as the v2 efficiency issue.
  • The RISC-vs-CISC ISA-granularity choice reshapes lowering granularity and the achievable overlap, so it is coupled to the ISA revision axis.

docs.title: Cost / geometry design rationale (measured)
docs.text:
These findings come from a cycle cost-model (sim/cost/) and cost snapshots that were later removed from git (commit 116b2b1); the measured conclusions that justify the shipped geometry are recorded here so they are not lost. Caveats: the cost model had ZERO ISS coupling (programs were hand-translated, not compiled .s), and flags[5]=async was a sim no-op, so absolute numbers are directional.

  • 2 DMA channels land within ~20% of 4 under per-burst arbitration -> the proposal went 4ch -> 2ch fair-share AXI.
  • Post-Gap1+2 the regime is compute-bound (async DMA fully hides under compute) - which is exactly why the missing scheduler/async path (above) leaves throughput on the table.
  • Target systolic width is fp16 DIM_FP=8 (~192 DSP on ZCU102) vs the current bitstream DIM=4. At M=1 decode, DIM=4 is ~1.75x the cycles of DIM=8 (not 4x).
  • vec_lane is the surprise bottleneck: 16 -> 32 buys ~+21% throughput.
  • GQA fusion saves ~5.9x DRAM bytes but ~0 wall-clock when compute-bound.
  • Tokens/sec trajectory: status-quo 0.078 -> Gap1+2+GQA+vl32(2ch) 0.136 (~1.74x); ASIC ceiling 0.883. Hotspots: decode_attn 53%, gate/up/down 41%.

Net: the shipped choices (DIM=8, vec_lane=32 target, 2-channel DMA) are the cost-model sweet spot, and the biggest un-banked win is the scheduling/async pass.

docs.title: NPU ISA - "v1.x freeze" (non-normative narrative)
docs.text:
Version axes - do not conflate. "v1.1/v1.2/v1.x freeze" here is a spec codename for this narrative's design-decision history, not the shipped package version. The package's machine-readable version is mininpu.isa.opcodes.ISA_VERSION (currently 0.4.0, pre-1.0), which is the only version number an importer should trust.

Normative SSOT: mininpu/isa/opcodes.py. All normative ISA definitions - opcode values + per-opcode semantics, FIELDS_* bit layouts, the D7 flags map, capacity constants, the frozen DMA descriptor caps, and the Numerical Precision Contracts (tolerance tiers + per-opcode valid input domains + bit-exact list) - live exclusively in mininpu/isa/opcodes.py as structured module/section/function docstrings and comments adjacent to the constants and encoders they define. This document is non-normative: rationale, the annotated FlashAttention assembly walkthrough, worked examples, and historical narrative only. It contains no opcode-value table, no encoding bit-layout table, no flags map, no capacity numbers, and no precision-tolerance table - those were removed (W10, per the v1.x ISA freeze) so there is no hand-maintained duplication. If anything here conflicts with mininpu/isa/opcodes.py, opcodes.py wins; update it first.

This structural single-SSOT consolidation is what makes the opcode-set / FLUSH-value divergence class (D3 / D6) impossible: there is exactly one place to change. (OQ-7: divergence is made impossible, not policed by an equality CI.)

Changelog (historical narrative - kept verbatim for provenance): - v1.1 (M4a): vec.rsqrt, vec.silu, vec.mul/add/sub.acc, vec.scale.col.bcast, cast.f16.f32, matmul.tile causal flag, multi-iv DMA, iv-dependent loop bounds, NPU_PROFILE. (Note: multi-iv DMA and iv-dependent loop bounds were removed/deferred by the v1.x freeze below - D2b/D4. This line is historical.) - v1.2 (M4b): vec.fma.imm (0x27), vec.add.vreg (0x28), vec.mul.vreg (0x29), vec.copy.acc (0x2A), cast.f16.f32.vreg (0x32). - v1.x freeze: iv/iv_stride arity capped at 1 (D2b; MultiLinearAddr deferred to v1.3+); loop bounds are literal-only (D4; scaled/iv-derived bounds removed, per-pid_m bound set by host IRAM-patch); FLUSH moved into the control-group nibble (D6, was 0xFF); normative flags map adopted (D7); flush drains async DMA on hardware / sim no-op, flags[5]=async reserved (D9); SPM/ACC clarified as a logical dtype partition over one unified byte-pool (D1). All these landed normatively in mininpu/isa/opcodes.py; this bullet records the decision history. vec.copy.acc (0x2A) was subsequently removed (D3/OQ-5) - the compiler lowers it to vec.scale.imm × 1.0; this changelog line is left intact as provenance.

Minimal hybrid ISA for a custom AI accelerator targeting tiled GEMM and online-softmax FlashAttention. Designed to be extensible toward CISC-style macro execution on RTL hardware. The exact encoding, opcode set, hardware model and numerical contracts are defined in mininpu/isa/opcodes.py (open that file to see both the encoding and its authoritative spec in one place); the material below explains why the design is shaped this way and how a representative kernel maps onto it.

docs.title: Why a fixed 64-bit word + literal pool
docs.text: The instruction word is deliberately a single fixed-width 64-bit value with only three operand slots. This keeps the RTL decoder trivial (no variable-length fetch, no operand-count state machine) at the cost of two operand classes that do not fit inline: wide values (full DRAM byte addresses, dram_stride, fp32 immediates) and extra operands (instructions with more than three operands, e.g. vec.fma). Both spill to a per-program literal pool emitted after the program body; the operand slot then holds a pool index and a D7 IMM_OP* flag bit marks the indirection. fp32 immediates are stored bit-exact (IEEE-754 pattern) so no precision is lost crossing the 16-bit operand boundary. The exact field positions, flag-bit assignments and pool mechanics are normative in mininpu/isa/opcodes.py (FIELDS_INSTR, the flags-byte docstring, LiteralPool).

docs.title: Why DMA is kept in an inline form
docs.text:
The DMA opcodes carry an inline (dst, dram_addr, mat_rows, mat_cols, dram_stride) form rather than exposing hardware descriptor registers directly. This keeps kernel assembly readable and lets the compiler reason about DMA shape symbolically; the adopted assembler.py (W6) lowers the inline form into RTL descriptor-config + trigger words at the npu-translate boundary. The frozen descriptor caps (mat_rows, mat_cols, dram_stride) are a hardware limit, enforced by the encoders in mininpu/isa/opcodes.py (they raise rather than silently truncate, which would corrupt the DMA shape).

dram_stride=0 broadcast encoding: dram_stride=0 is a valid encoding for dma.load.spm (and the typed variants) meaning "broadcast": every row of the SPM tile is loaded from the same DRAM base address. The simulator implements this by repeating the single row mat_rows times. The primary use case is broadcasting a 1-row bias vector to BLOCK_M rows for a bias-add without requiring a dedicated opcode or host-side tensor replication.

docs.title: Why SPM and ACC are one physical pool (D1)
docs.text: "SPM" and "ACC" are not separate memories - they are the fp16 and fp32 views of one unified on-chip byte-addressed scratch. Keeping them logically distinct in the ISA preserves clean instruction semantics (a matmul writes "ACC", a cast writes "SPM") while the allocator treats the pool as a single byte-weighted budget. ACC_ROWS is therefore a convenience figure, not an independent hardware budget. This is a clarification only; instruction semantics are unchanged. The capacity numbers and the unified-pool sizes are normative in mininpu/isa/opcodes.py.

docs.title: Why loop bounds are literal-only (D4)
docs.text: Earlier drafts allowed iv-derived / scaled loop bounds so the causal FlashAttention phase-1 bound (pid_m × BLOCK_N) could be computed in hardware at loop entry. The freeze removes this: hardware does no multiply/address arithmetic at loop entry, the bound is a literal, and the host IRAM-patches the per-pid_m value before each grid launch via the patch table (a compiler↔abi.py contract; see mininpu/abi.py). This trades a tiny amount of host work for a much simpler sequencer and makes one program serve every grid launch. The literal-only loop encoding and the single-iv (arity-1, D2b) loop-body DMA address mode are normative in mininpu/isa/opcodes.py.

docs.title: Why flush is the only ordering primitive (D9)
docs.text: There is no async-DMA programming model in frozen v1.x, so a single global flush fence is the sole ordering primitive; flags[5]=async is reserved with no v1.x semantics. flush drains in-flight async DMA on hardware and is a no-op in the functional simulator. It must precede any off-chip store a host will read. The opcode value and these semantics are normative in mininpu/isa/opcodes.py.

docs.title: FlashAttention forward - annotated assembly (worked example)
docs.text: This is the canonical worked example showing how the opcode set composes into a real kernel. It is illustrative, not a normative encoding reference - every mnemonic below is defined normatively in mininpu/isa/opcodes.py.

docs.title: Causal variant (M4a canonical form)
docs.text:
# Inputs:  Q[BLOCK_M, HEAD_DIM], K[N_CTX, HEAD_DIM], V[N_CTX, HEAD_DIM] fp16 in DRAM
# Output:  O[BLOCK_M, HEAD_DIM] fp16 in DRAM
# Params:  qk_scale = sm_scale * log2(e)  (base-2 exp trick)
# Grid:    outer loops over pid_m (M-blocks) and heads (not shown here)
#
# Two-phase structure:
#   Phase 1 - full blocks below the diagonal (n < pid_m * BLOCK_M): no mask
#   Phase 2 - diagonal block (n = pid_m * BLOCK_M):          causal=1 mask

    dma.load.spm    q,  Q_ADDR,  BLOCK_M, HEAD_DIM, HEAD_DIM*2
    vec.movi        mi, -inf,    BLOCK_M
    vec.movi        li,  1.0,    BLOCK_M
    vec.zero.acc    acc, BLOCK_M, HEAD_DIM

    ; ── Phase 1: non-diagonal K/V blocks ────────────────────────────────────
    ; upper bound is a literal placeholder (D4); the host IRAM-patches it to
    ; pid_m*BLOCK_N per grid launch via the abi.py patch table (FREEZE §10).
    loop.begin  n, 0, HI_PATCH, BLOCK_N        ; HI_PATCH = literal, host-patched
        dma.load.spm    k, K_ADDR  iv=n  iv_stride=HEAD_DIM*2  BLOCK_N, HEAD_DIM, HEAD_DIM*2
        dma.load.spm    v, V_ADDR  iv=n  iv_stride=HEAD_DIM*2  BLOCK_N, HEAD_DIM, HEAD_DIM*2

        matmul.tile     qk, q, k,  transB=1, accumulate=0      ; qk = q @ k^T
        vec.scale.imm   qk, qk, qk_scale

        vec.rowmax      mij_raw, qk
        vec.maximum     mij, mi, mij_raw

        vec.sub.bcast   p, qk, mij
        vec.exp2        p, p

        vec.rowsum      lij, p
        vec.sub.vreg    mdiff, mi, mij
        vec.exp2.vreg   alpha, mdiff

        vec.scale.bcast acc, acc, alpha
        cast.f32.f16    p_fp16, p
        matmul.tile     acc, p_fp16, v,  accumulate=1

        vec.fma         li, alpha, li, lij
        vec.copy.vreg   mi, mij
    loop.end n

    ; ── Phase 2: diagonal block with causal mask ─────────────────────────────
    dma.load.spm    k, K_ADDR  iv=pid_m  iv_stride=HEAD_DIM*2  BLOCK_N, HEAD_DIM, HEAD_DIM*2
    dma.load.spm    v, V_ADDR  iv=pid_m  iv_stride=HEAD_DIM*2  BLOCK_N, HEAD_DIM, HEAD_DIM*2

    matmul.tile     qk, q, k,  transB=1, accumulate=0, causal=1  ; upper-tri → -INF
    vec.scale.imm   qk, qk, qk_scale

    vec.rowmax      mij_raw, qk
    vec.maximum     mij, mi, mij_raw

    vec.sub.bcast   p, qk, mij
    vec.exp2        p, p

    vec.rowsum      lij, p
    vec.sub.vreg    mdiff, mi, mij
    vec.exp2.vreg   alpha, mdiff

    vec.scale.bcast acc, acc, alpha
    cast.f32.f16    p_fp16, p
    matmul.tile     acc, p_fp16, v,  accumulate=1

    vec.fma         li, alpha, li, lij

    ; ── Tail ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    vec.div.bcast   acc_norm, acc, li
    cast.f32.f16    o_fp16, acc_norm
    dma.store.spm   O_ADDR, o_fp16
    flush

The two-phase structure is the key composition idea: every non-diagonal K/V block runs the online-softmax update with no mask; only the single diagonal block sets matmul.tile causal=1, which masks the tile-local upper triangle to -INF post-multiply. The loop's upper bound is a literal placeholder that the host patches per pid_m launch (D4) - the program itself is launch-invariant.

docs.title: Worked example - physical assembly form
docs.text:
The .s file format (M3.5c and later) carries both symbolic handles and physical bank indices. Physical kwargs are mandatory in compiler-emitted programs; parse_asm validates them against an independent allocator run and raises ValueError if compiler and sim disagree on placement:

matmul.tile  r0  r1  r2  transB=1  accumulate=0  acc_row=0  spm_a_row=256  spm_b_row=512
vec.movi  r3  imm=-3.4028235e+38  rows=64  vreg=3

Test harnesses use mininpu.iss.dram.DRAMAllocator to assign DRAM addresses within the partitioned address space (defined normatively in mininpu/isa/opcodes.py) rather than hard-coding values.

docs.title: Completed
docs.text:
• Triton frontend (M4a): triton-shared-opt --triton-to-linalg-experimental
  • extended LinalgToNPU patterns for the 7 M4a/M4b atom kernels (GEMM, FA-fwd causal GQA, RMSNorm, RMSNorm-multitile, SwiGLU, RoPE, KV-cache append).
  • Transformer-block composition (M4b): 7/7 Triton atom kernels pass on both prod and tutorial profiles. ISA v1.2 additions: vec.fma.imm, vec.add/mul.vreg, vec.copy.acc, cast.f16.f32.vreg (all now normative in mininpu/isa/opcodes.py; vec.copy.acc subsequently lowered, D3).
  • TinyLlama-1.1B e2e (M4c): 22-layer greedy decode; HF weight bridge (safetensors, bf16→fp16); token-exact match vs HuggingFace float32 confirmed.
  • Decode-step attention (N=1): a host-padded [1,D]→[BLOCK_M,D] kernel reusing the FA path, zero ISA change (per the v1.x freeze). Shipped as the decode_attn atom kernel and validated end-to-end (test_decode_attn).

docs.title: Planned
docs.text:
• New scalar activation (GELU, LayerNorm): vec.gelu is reserved (opcode allocated in mininpu/isa/opcodes.py, unimplemented - OQ-8); activating it needs one encoder + one handlers.py entry. No other changes.
  • Backward pass: needs vec.scale.bcast, vec.rowsum, vec.exp2.vreg (already present) plus one new dma.atomic.add for gradient accumulation (deferred, no v1.x reservation - OQ-8).

docs.title: Known gaps (not yet implemented)
docs.text:
1. Tile-aligned only - non-tile-aligned shapes require host-side padding; partial-tile masking is the host's responsibility.
  2. Binary encoding: the 64-bit fixed-width encoding is defined in mininpu/isa/opcodes.py; the compiler adopts assembler.py as the binary producer (W6 / D5 reversal - ISA known-gap #2 closes there).
  3. No address descriptor registers - loop-body DMA addresses use inline LinearAddr expressions; the adopted assembler.py lowers them to hardware descriptor + trigger words.
  4. No int8 / quantized mode - int8 opcodes are reserved-only in mininpu/isa/opcodes.py (D10); not wired into DMA or matmul.
  5. No async DMA / multi-channel barriers - only one global flush fence; flags[5]=async reserved (D9).
  6. No conditional control flow - only counted loop.begin/loop.end.
  7. Address space encoded per-opcode, not per-type - adding a fourth memory space requires a new opcode rather than a memref type attribute.
  8. No multi-tenant / process-id support.
  9. Causal mask is matmul-only - applied post-multiply inside matmul.tile; no standalone vector causal-mask op.
  10. Loop bounds are literal-only (D4) - per-pid_m variation is realized by host IRAM-patch of the literal (compiler↔abi.py patch-table contract, FREEZE §10), not by an ISA semantic. MultiLinearAddr (iv-list length > 1 in loop-body DMA) is deferred to v1.3+ (D2b).
